steps.title: Spot the decisive evidence
steps.content: The O157:H7 test turned purple while all others turned pink, showing that the aptamers bound specifically to their target and rejected all non-targets in the real-world pear juice.

steps.title: Match the findings to the inference
steps.content:
D correctly concludes that the aptamers bound to O157:H7 and displayed high specificity in real-world food.

A, B, and C misframe these results—either comparing different target pathogens, failing to see that results resolve rather than explain prior uncertainty, or claiming binding occurred to non-targets.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
